blocks.content: Although retired from active duty, the influence of a first-class noble was immense. Many parents approached him to greet him.
Shirone led his friends to Shakora and took the opportunity to greet him when other nobles weren't around.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hello.

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: Ah, Shirone. How have you been?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, thanks to you.
Isis approached with a worried expression.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Shirone, how is our Amy? Do you think she can pass?

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: How would Shirone know that? Don't stress the boy out,
Shakora scolded, making Isis pout.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! It's okay. I saw her yesterday, and she seemed in good condition.

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: Hmm, that's a relief.
Shakora's face showed a hint of relief. Despite trying to appear calm, he was clearly nervous.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even a first-class noble was just a parent when it came to their child.
Iruki and Neide, who were standing behind Shirone, also greeted Shakora.

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: So, you're Iruki. How is your father?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's... still around, I guess.
It was a cheeky remark for such an occasion, but Shakora just laughed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Honestly, Iruki was quite polite compared to his notoriously eccentric father.

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: Shirone, bring your friends over to our house sometime.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I will.
After finishing their greetings, Shakora and Isis headed to the entrance for parents.
Shirone entered the Colosseum through the student entrance.
Although juniors and classmates gave him wary looks, he didn't feel lonely.
Students who were only interested in magic, not status, gradually gathered around Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After all, to discuss the exam, you needed to be on the same level.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: How does it feel to send your girlfriend to the battlefield?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know yet. What do you think?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: There seems to be a difference in skill levels among the participants. Based on objective abilities, she should be in the top ten. But the exam subjects will play a big role. Unexpected situations usually arise from asymmetrical power.
As an information processing major, Dante's analysis was hard to argue with.
While they talked, Sabina waved at Neide.

blocks.speaker: Sabina
blocks.content: Hi.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Oh, hi.
They weren't close enough to warrant a special greeting, so Neide responded politely.
Sabina stood there for a while, waiting for more conversation, but when Neide showed no interest, she sulkily sat next to Dante.
Pandora's scoff was audible.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Everyone, it's starting.
The 30 students participating in the graduation exam stood in a line in the center of the Colosseum, all wearing 2000-series bracelets.
However, the audience didn't need bracelets to watch. The exam venue was designed to the highest national standards, making the entire Colosseum a giant Image Zone.
Arin clasped her hands and prayed for Amy's success.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Do you think that helps? If prayers worked, anyone could become a mage.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Besides, you don't even believe in God.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Still, I'm nervous. Don't you want Amy to pass?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Why should I care who passes? It's not my exam.
Arin glared at Canis, feeling hurt.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: But his indifference was only on the surface.
As he rested his chin on his hand, watching the arena, he saw Amy, ready for battle, and smiled slightly.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Do your best, pumpkin.
The faculty, led by Alpheas and Olivia, took their seats.
Parents sat on the left, while scouts and reporters sat on the right.
After Alpheas's opening speech, Olivia explained the exam evaluation criteria.
The exam was divided into two parts, with two out of six subjects chosen by roulette.
Since they didn't know which subjects would be chosen, students had to train in all areas.
This rule was established to ensure fairness for non-combat mages and to give repeaters a slight advantage, as a courtesy from the Magic Association.
The preliminary event ended with the students pledging to compete fairly.
The 30 participants stood in a circle along the Colosseum wall, and the announcer, Saad, shouted from the podium.

blocks.speaker: Saad
blocks.content: We will now begin the Alpheas Magic School graduation exam!
A giant circular hologram appeared in the sky above the Colosseum.
The six subjects were evenly divided, and the central pointer began to spin rapidly.
The participants clenched their fists and performed the water ceremony.
The 2000-series would activate the moment the pointer stopped. They had to move instantly.
Finally, the pointer indicated the first exam subject.
Wow!
The crowd's cheers filled the Colosseum.
Night fell.
People poured out of the Colosseum's exit, all with excited faces.
Shirone's group also remained silent until they were outside.
At the exit, Neide turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: What now, Shirone? Iruki and I are hungry and need to eat something.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Go ahead. I'll stay with Amy.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Alright. See you tomorrow.
After his friends left, Shirone exhaled white breath, warming his cold insides.
He turned as light from the Colosseum shone on the scoreboard displaying the names of the successful candidates.
The top ten students had become mages and were ready to venture into the world.
1st: Alt Amira
2nd: Ludwig Aconel
3rd: Connie Kale
4th: Tanto Selena
5th: Ekins Samuel
6th: Aresa Depo
7th: Fortress Seriel
8th: Lara Coril
9th: Andres Raul
10th: Dolin Rickston
Amy's name was not there.
She had been the third to be eliminated as soon as the first exam began.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The first evaluation was combat power measurement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since it favored combat mages, the students focused on targeting Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was practically a one-sided lynching.
Seriel couldn't even intervene before Amy's mental gauge hit zero.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It defied expert analysis, but such variables were part of the graduation exam.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy must have anticipated this and prepared thoroughly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Still, it felt off.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Collusion in combat is natural.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When one side grows stronger, the weaker ones band together.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But did it just seem like the strong were colluding?
Initially, it was chaotic.
Amy wasn't the only one aspiring to be a combat mage, and with 30 participants, the firepower was dispersed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But when Fermi's group started acting, the tide turned sharply.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They targeted only Amy, avoiding all other students' attacks.
After about a minute, even the initially unaware students realized that siding with Fermi's group made things easier.
Eventually, everyone except Seriel attacked Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even the mighty Amy couldn't withstand such an onslaught.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fermi's group's combat power had to be superior to the combined power of all other participants for such a tactic to work.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could this be called natural collusion?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The strangest part was that none of Fermi's group passed the exam.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It even seemed like they deliberately lost to Samuel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Samuel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Most students noticed that Fermi's group seemed to support Samuel during the first exam.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But the feeling was so subtle that it was hard to judge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The harshness of the graduation exam?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There were too many oddities to dismiss it as that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Most suspicious was that Fermi, who had never lost first place in six years, was at the center of it all.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maybe it's just a misunderstanding.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's no guarantee such things won't happen on the battlefield.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And even if it's not a misunderstanding, what can I do about it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Simply put, the stronger side won the combat power measurement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if it was the result of artificial collusion, it didn't change the evaluation criteria.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Unless there was a personal deal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There could have been an agreement between Fermi and Samuel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Shirone quickly dismissed the thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it wasn't true, it would be a great insult to Samuel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Besides, if such a thing had happened, the faculty would have already investigated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah, no matter how much I think about it, there's nothing I can do.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Right now, he was only worried about Amy.
Since she was eliminated early, her mental strength should have recovered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But thinking about how she had to watch the exam from the waiting room for a long time made his heart ache.
Amy's parents emerged from the parents' exit.
Shirone approached them, unsure of what expression to make.

blocks.speaker: Isis
blocks.content: Shirone...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone...

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: Watch your words. How do you think Amy would feel hearing that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He also found some parts suspicious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But nothing was so strange as to be beyond reason.
Except for Fermi's group's elimination, the battlefield's flow was rational and logical.
And since the second exam was a non-combat subject, Fermi's group's elimination couldn't be deemed unnatural.
Shirone swallowed dryly as he saw the eliminated students leaving.
When he saw Amy among the middle group, his mind went blank.
Amy glanced at Shirone before approaching her parents.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hehe, Dad, Mom. Sorry, I failed spectacularly.

blocks.speaker: Shakora
blocks.content: It's just an exam. You did your best, so don't be too disappointed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Disappointed? No way. I think I made a mistake in my judgment.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'll just have to try again next year.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt relieved by Amy's bright demeanor but also worried.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She couldn't be feeling normal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She was putting her family's feelings first.
Now the successful candidates were coming out.
Seriel approached Amy, crying her eyes out.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Amy, Amy...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Congratulations, Seriel. Do your best for both of us. I'll catch up soon.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Why wasn't it Amy instead of me?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Anyone could see that Amy should have been the one to pass.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She should have realized it sooner.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: If she had quickly sided with Amy when Fermi's group attacked, the battle might have turned out differently.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Amy... I...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Seriel, get a grip. You passed the exam.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Do you understand? You took your graduation exam, and I took mine. And you passed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Nothing else matters.
Seriel swallowed her tears.
She couldn't cry. How could she cry when Amy wasn't?
